_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to examine different methods of civic participation in issues at local, state, and national levels, and explain why and how people use them.

_source.w: We are learning to examine different methods of civic participation in issues at local, state, and national levels, and explain why and how people use them.
y: Understanding the range of participation options helps us choose the most effective way to make our voice heard.
Different issues and contexts call for different methods of civic action.
Examining how others participate inspires us to find our own ways to contribute to positive change.
eg: Researching a local campaign and examining the methods used — petitions, social media, community meetings.
Comparing the effectiveness of different civic participation methods for different types of issues.
Investigating how a national advocacy campaign brought about change in government policy.
Examining how Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities have used civic participation to advance rights.
Evaluating the role of digital activism versus traditional methods of civic engagement.
Planning our own civic participation strategy for a school or community issue.
